summary: Tested Super Admin Panel loading fix. The core bug (infinite spinner) is confirmed fixed. try/finally in loadData ensures setLoading(false) is called even on errors. Panel loads within ~8 seconds showing correct metrics (3 clients, active count, 21 users, 4 plans). All 13 backend tests pass. Feature flag management, New Client wizard (4-step), and client user access control all working correctly.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 - minor REST convention deviation

issues: Super admin gets redirected to /procurement after login (canAccessDashboard returns false for super_admin role), which then fails to load data and shows 'Failed to load data' toast twice
Fix: DashboardRoute should redirect super_admin to /platform-admin instead of /procurement
Root cause: dashboardAccessRoles array in moduleConfig.js does not include 'super_admin'

panel_loading_fix.details: try/finally in loadData ensures setLoading(false) is always called. Panel loads from spinner state to full data in ~8s

metrics_display.details: Shows Total Clients (3), Active (3), Total Users (21), Plans Active (4) - all correct

tenant_list.details: Lists all tenants: Ocean Fresh Exports, Blue Ocean Exports, TEST Coastal Seafoods with Modules/View buttons

new_client_wizard.details: 4-step wizard opens with steps: Company, Owner, Modules, Review. Step 1 form inputs visible. Next button enabled after filling required fields.

feature_flag_management.details: Modules modal shows 13 feature toggles. Toggle click changes state (border-purple-500/border-gray-200). PUT API call succeeds and persists changes.

client_user_access_control.details: Client user (john@aquapremium.com/admin role) does NOT see Super Admin nav link. Direct navigation to /platform-admin results in redirect to /. Backend returns 403 for all super-admin endpoints with client token.

super_admin_sidebar_link.details: [data-testid='nav-super-admin'] present and visible only for super_admin role. Super Admin Debug console shows isAccessible:true for super_admin role.

action_items: MINOR UX FIX: In DashboardRoute (App.js), add special handling for super_admin role to redirect to /platform-admin instead of /procurement. This prevents the 'Failed to load data' toast after login.
critical_code_review_comments: try/finally in loadData (SuperAdminPanel.js lines 98-107) correctly ensures loading spinner resolves - BUG FIX CONFIRMED WORKING
SuperAdminPanel is now directly imported in App.js (no lazy loading) - prevents Suspense delay issues
DashboardRoute does not handle super_admin role - super_admin redirects to /procurement which causes API errors and 'Failed to load data' toast. Minor but noticeable UX issue.
Client user cannot directly access /platform-admin - React Router redirects them to / root. Additionally, backend properly blocks with 403.

success_rate.frontend: 95% - All core features working. Minor: 'Failed to load data' toast on super_admin login due to /procurement redirect

seed_data_creation: Created TEST Coastal Seafoods tenant (test_tenant_*) via backend tests. This tenant will persist in the database.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Super Admin Panel is fully working. Loading fix (try/finally) confirmed. Only remaining issue: super_admin gets 'Failed to load data' toast because DashboardRoute redirects them to /procurement on login. To fix: add super_admin special case in DashboardRoute in App.js. All backend APIs working at 100%. Frontend panel loads in ~8 seconds (Cloudflare delay in automated browser).

failed_to_load_data_toast.issue: Super admin sees 'Failed to load data' toast after login
failed_to_load_data_toast.root_cause: Login calls navigate('/') which triggers DashboardRoute. canAccessDashboard('super_admin') returns false (super_admin not in dashboardAccessRoles). So DashboardRoute redirects to /procurement. Procurement.js then calls the procurement API with the super_admin user's token. The API returns empty/error data for super_admin (no tenant_id), causing the error toast.
failed_to_load_data_toast.fix: Add: if (user?.role === 'super_admin') return <Navigate to='/platform-admin' replace />; before the existing redirect to /procurement
failed_to_load_data_toast.priority: MINOR - does not affect core functionality, only UX
